name: Hattie''s Venison Chili Con Carne
recipeInstructions:
1. Take 2 lbs of venison, either as meat or venison burger. If using meat, cube it and brown in batches in olive oil (about 2 tbsp) in a large skillet (I prefer a ten-inch cast iron skillet - not Caphalon!). If using venison burger, just brown it. Drain the meat.
2. In a heavy pot with a lid (mine is a Le Creuset purchased at the Salvation Army in Santa Cruz, CA), heat 2 tbsp olive oil and sauté two large onions that have been sliced.
3. When the onions are limp but not yellow (don't overcook them for about 7 minutes total), add 10 cloves of minced garlic. Scents up the garlic, then add the meat. Add 1 quart of homemade chicken or beef stock. If you don't have your own stock, use Campbell's low-sodium chicken stock.
4. Add the spices: crush oregano in hand before adding.
5. Bring to a boil, reduce to a simmer, cover, and simmer for at least three hours or until the meat is fork-tender.
6. Even though venison is very lean, defat the chili by letting it cool and refrigerating overnight. The next day, remove the fat.
7. To serve, reheat. Some like to thicken the chili with masa or fine corn meal. Mix about 1/4 cup fine corn meal with enough water to make a thin paste (no more than one cup of water). Quickly add this to the chili as it simmers and stir vigorously to avoid lumps. If using masa, cook the chili for an additional 20 minutes or so.
8. Serve in bowls with rice, beans, bread, or other sides. Coleslaw is a suitable salad option.
9. This recipe freezes beautifully.
